outbound_official.notes: Official MFA page titled 'Visa requirements for citizens of the Republic of Uzbekistan' (O'zbekiston Respublikasi fuqarolari uchun viza talablari). Page last updated 2025-12-29. Kenya, Côte d'Ivoire, Seychelles, and Sri Lanka listed under visa-free but may require tourist voucher/hotel confirmation and return ticket. Jordan added to visa-free per Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025. Qatar and Saudi Arabia appear as visa-on-arrival on this page (consistent with the December 2025 snapshot). Page is in Russian/Uzbek; no equivalent English-language page was retrievable from gov.uz.

visa_free.notes: Upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025 under the Uzbekistan-China mutual visa-free agreement signed December 1, 2024 in Chengdu (confirmed by Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025). Maximum 90 days within any 180-day period. IMPORTANT: Does NOT apply to visits to Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR — those require a separate visa. Does not cover work, study, or media activities.
Upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025, confirmed in force by Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025.
Upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025, confirmed in force by Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025.
Upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025, confirmed in force by Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025.
Was 10-day visa-free (missing from prior record). Extended to 30-day visa-free under Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025.
Added to 30-day visa-free regime under Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025. Previously eligible only for e-visa. Saudi Arabia is also eligible for e-visa via e-visa.gov.uz.
Added to 30-day visa-free regime under Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025. Previously required a visa.
Visa-free effective January 1, 2026, per Presidential Decree No. 203 of November 3, 2025. Now in force.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Visa on arrival available at Tashkent International Airport for citizens of countries without an Uzbekistan diplomatic mission in their country, requiring prior confirmation from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs

e_visa.notes: Single entry $20, double entry $35, multiple entry $50; e-visa valid 90 days from issuance
Also eligible for visa-free entry since June 2025
Includes Hong Kong SAR; note that Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR are excluded from the June 2025 mutual visa-free agreement and may still require e-visa
Listed as Macedonia in original UN source
Also eligible for visa-free entry since June 2025
Also eligible for visa-free entry since October 2025
Also eligible for visa-free entry since January 1, 2026

visa_policy.notes: CIS member states (Azerbaijan, Georgia, Armenia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Moldova, Russia, Ukraine) have unlimited visa-free stay; Kyrgyzstan 60 days; Tajikistan 30 days. Most of Europe, Americas and Asia-Pacific 30 days. China upgraded to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025 (mutual agreement) — excludes HK/Macao SAR. Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman upgraded to 30-day visa-free from June 1, 2025. Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Jordan added at 30-day visa-free under Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025. USA visa-free from January 1, 2026 (Presidential Decree No. 203, Nov 3, 2025) — now in force. E-visa (e-visa.gov.uz) available for 76+ countries at USD 20 single entry. Transit visa-free (5 days) for transit passengers on Uzbekistan Airways.

conditional_access.conditions: Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of China (up to 30 days). Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.
Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Hungary (up to 30 days). Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Turkmenistan (up to 30 days). Ordinary passport holders of Turkmenistan require a visa. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Vietnam (up to 60 days). Ordinary passport holders of Vietnam require a visa. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of India (up to 60 days). Ordinary passport holders of India require an e-visa. Note: accredited diplomats only (except India, which is excluded from the unlimited-period accredited-diplomat provision).

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of the Republic of Korea (up to 60 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 60-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Brazil (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Egypt (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders of Egypt require a visa. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Kuwait (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders of Kuwait are now visa-free for 30 days (upgraded June 2025); diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Latvia (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Note: some aggregator sources indicate this covers 'diplomatic and service passports' but official gov.uz page specifies 'diplomatic passports'. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of the UAE (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Poland (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Romania (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Note: some aggregator sources indicate this covers 'diplomatic and service passports' but official gov.uz page specifies 'diplomatic passports'. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Singapore (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Slovakia (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Note: some aggregator sources indicate this covers 'diplomatic and service passports' but official gov.uz page specifies 'diplomatic passports'. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Turkey (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Estonia (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Japan (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program exists. Discussions about a potential $1 million investment CBI program have occurred but as of 2026 no official program has been launched. Uzbekistan generally does not allow dual citizenship.

programs.name: Golden Visa — Donation Route
programs.notes: 5-year residence permit via one-off donation of USD 250,000 for main applicant; USD 150,000 per additional family member (spouse, children, parents). No nationality restrictions. No requirement to form a business or purchase real estate. Introduced by Presidential Decree No. UP-67 of April 18, 2025; effective June 1, 2025. Path to citizenship not confirmed officially.

programs.name: Residence Permit via Business Investment — Standard
programs.notes: 3-year renewable residence permit for investment of USD 250,000 in a company registered in Uzbekistan. Based on amendments to Presidential Decree No. UP-5611.

programs.name: Residence Permit via Business Investment — Large Scale
programs.notes: 10-year renewable residence permit for investment of USD 3,000,000 in a business engaged in production of goods or services. Based on amendments to Presidential Decree No. UP-5611.

programs.name: Residence Permit via Real Estate — Tashkent/Tashkent Region
programs.notes: Residence permit via real estate investment of USD 300,000 in Tashkent city or Tashkent region. Based on amendments to Presidential Decree No. UP-5611.

programs.name: Residence Permit via Real Estate — Major Cities
programs.notes: Residence permit via real estate investment of USD 200,000 in cities of Samarkand, Bukhara, Namangan, Andijan, Fergana, or Khorezm. Based on amendments to Presidential Decree No. UP-5611.

programs.name: Residence Permit via Real Estate — Other Regions
programs.notes: Residence permit via real estate investment of USD 100,000 in other regions including Republic of Karakalpakstan. Based on amendments to Presidential Decree No. UP-5611.

programs.name: Investment Visa (3-year)
programs.eligibility: Foreign investors who have made an investment of USD 3 million or more in Uzbekistan
programs.notes: 3-year investor visa for foreign investors. Confirmed on Embassy of Uzbekistan in the United States official site.

validation_notes: CORRECTION: China (CHN) upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free. Mutual Uzbekistan-China visa-free agreement signed December 1, 2024 in Chengdu, entered into force June 1, 2025 (source: gov.uz/en/mfa/news/view/53659). Confirmed by Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025. Maximum stay 30 days per entry, 90 days within any 180-day period. Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR are explicitly EXCLUDED from this agreement.
CORRECTION: Bahrain (BHR) upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025. Prior record had incorrect note 'proposed but not yet confirmed as enacted' — the extension is now in force.
CORRECTION: Kuwait (KWT) upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025. Same as Bahrain — prior record incorrectly marked as 'proposed'.
CORRECTION: Oman (OMN) upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025. Same correction as Bahrain and Kuwait.

ADDITION: Qatar (QAT) added to visa_free list at 30 days. Was previously listed at 10-day visa-free in official sources (alongside Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, China) but was MISSING from the prior record entirely. Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025 extended Qatar to 30 days.

ADDITION: Saudi Arabia (SAU) added to visa_free list at 30 days. Was previously only in e_visa list. Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025 granted Saudi Arabia ordinary-passport visa-free access for 30 days.
ADDITION: Jordan (JOR) added to visa_free list at 30 days. Completely missing from prior record. Added by Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025.
CONFIRMED: USA (USA) visa-free from January 1, 2026 per Presidential Decree No. 203 (Nov 3, 2025) — now in force as of validation date (June 2026). Notes updated to remove future-tense language.
CONFIRMED: Tajikistan (TJK) is correctly 30 days (not 60 days) — confirmed by current official source.
NOTE: The China e_visa entry note updated to clarify that Hong Kong SAR holders may still require e-visa as HK/Macao SAR are excluded from the mutual visa-free agreement.
gaps: The complete official enumeration of all e-visa eligible countries (full Appendix 1 referenced in gov.uz decree) could not be directly accessed as e-visa.gov.uz renders dynamically; the list has reportedly expanded to 76+ countries as of 2025.
Max stay days for visa-on-arrival not confirmed from official source — only described as available with prior MFA confirmation.
Path to permanent residency and citizenship from RBI programs not confirmed from official source.

Whether Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR holders require e-visa or standard visa was not confirmed from a directly-fetched official source; the China mutual agreement explicitly excludes them, suggesting they still need e-visa or regular visa.

The Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025 source was confirmed only via Uzbek news site zamin.uz (not a *.gov.uz domain); a direct gov.uz URL for this resolution was not obtained. Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and Jordan entries are marked source_official: false as a result.
The 'My Second Home' 1-year tourist visa program — while announced — could not be confirmed with a direct official gov.uz URL.
Dual citizenship policy for RBI/golden visa holders not confirmed from official source.
CIS free movement: Uzbekistan is in CIS but not EAEU; the unlimited stay for certain CIS nationals is based on bilateral agreements not a formal CIS-wide freedom of movement treaty.
The updated 76-country e-visa list was not individually enumerated from an official source.

No official Uzbekistan government source found confirming any conditional entry rule based on holding a foreign visa (US, Schengen, UK, etc.) — credential-based conditional access appears absent from Uzbekistan's policy.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) or equivalent special diaspora/heritage status exemption found from any official Uzbekistan government source.

For Latvia, Romania, and Slovakia: some aggregator sources state 'diplomatic and service passports' are covered, but the official gov.uz page consistently states 'diplomatic passports' only. The service passport scope for these three countries could not be verified from a directly-fetched official source.

Bilateral visa-exemption agreements for diplomatic and service passports signed with Albania (September 2024) and others may be pending ratification and have not been recorded as in-force conditional access rules.
